data.title: Energous Jumps On Customer Expansion And Strong Deployment Momentum
data.description:
Customer Expansion

One Fortune 10 customer continues rolling out wireless power infrastructure across about 4,700 U.S. locations, with over 1,500 installations completed.

A second Fortune 10 customer has expanded its program across multiple use cases and geographies, including international markets, with more than 14 installations completed.

Pipeline And Technology

The company is also advancing proof-of-concept programs across retail, manufacturing, grocery, quick-service restaurants, and government sectors, with several initiatives expected to scale into broader deployments.

Energous said it is supporting multiple customer programs at different stages of commercialization.

Energous highlighted the strong performance of its wireless power technology in challenging environments, including refrigerated and temperature-controlled settings, supporting use cases in cold-chain, foodservice, and logistics.

The company has also added a second U.S.-based contract manufacturer to expand production capacity and meet domestic sourcing requirements.

Executive Commentary

"The momentum that defined 2025 has carried into early 2026," said CEO and CFO Mallorie Burak.

“The commercial infrastructure we built last year, recently expanded manufacturing capacity, a strengthened balance sheet, and a growing portfolio of active deployments, is enabling us to pursue opportunities at a pace and scale that was not possible twelve months ago.”

Separately, Energous plans to restart quarterly investor conference calls beginning with its first-quarter 2026 results in May.

Technical Analysis

Energous is currently trading near its 52-week high, reflecting a strong bullish trend. The stock is trading 36% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 67.1% above its 50-day SMA, indicating robust short-term momentum and a positive intermediate trend.

The relative strength index (RSI) is at 55.15, suggesting neutral momentum, while the moving average convergence divergence (MACD) is below the signal line, indicating potential bearish pressure.

This positioning suggests that while the stock is strong, traders should be cautious of potential pullbacks.

  • Key Resistance: $22.00 — This level may act as a barrier for further upward movement.
  • Key Support: $20.50 — A drop below this level could indicate weakening momentum.

Energous has shown impressive performance over the past 12 months, with a return of 172.09%. This significant gain highlights the stock’s strong upward trajectory and investor confidence in its growth potential.

WATT Stock Price Activity: Energous shares were up 37.65% at $22.57 at the time of publication on Wednesday. The stock is trading at a new 52-week high, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo: Shutterstock

data.title: Bezos Just Bought What Musk Wanted — And The $200B Space War Is Escalating
data.description:
And according to Bank of America, the prize isn't small: a $200 billion low-Earth orbit (LEO) market spanning broadband, wireless, and defense.

The Deal Musk Didn't Get

Officially, SpaceX never confirmed it was bidding. Unofficially, the industry chatter says otherwise.

Satellite analyst Tim Farrar reported that Globalstar's chairman shopped Amazon's offer to SpaceX to spark a bidding war—one where Elon Musk's company was widely seen as the frontrunner just weeks ago.

That aligns with what insiders were saying at SatShow 2026, where a potential SpaceX-Globalstar tie-up was the talk of the show, with executives pointing to SpaceX's aggressive push into direct-to-cell (D2C) spectrum as a natural fit.

Even market signals hinted at a duel. Globalstar shares jumped on reports that both Amazon and SpaceX were deep in due diligence, while SpaceX's last-minute, incomplete FCC auction filing was widely read as a strategic placeholder during parallel negotiations.

In the end, Jeff Bezos didn't just win—he likely forced the price higher.

Why Globalstar Matters Now

This isn't about satellites—it's about spectrum control.

Globalstar owns valuable L-band and MSS spectrum, the backbone for satellite-to-phone connectivity. That's the next frontier, where smartphones connect directly to space—no towers required.

Amazon's move neatly complements its Project Kuiper ambitions, while also aligning with Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL), which already relies on Globalstar for emergency SOS features.

The $200 Billion Orbit Grab

Zoom out, and this is bigger than Bezos vs. Musk.

Musk’s Starlink still dominates on scale. But Amazon just closed a critical gap—locking in spectrum, partnerships, and positioning in one move.

Call it what it is: this wasn't just an acquisition.

It was a block.

And if this deal is any indication, the space race isn't just back—it's getting crowded, expensive, and a lot more personal.

Image created using artificial intelligence via Midjourney.

data.title: Quantum Threat Gains Attention In Crypto As Experts Warn Of Long-Term Risk
data.description:
For much of crypto's history, the idea that quantum computing could undermine blockchain security has lived on the fringes of the conversation. It was acknowledged, but rarely treated as an urgent concern. That is beginning to change.

A growing number of researchers, developers, and institutional analysts are now revisiting the quantum threat with greater seriousness, particularly as advances in computing power continue to accelerate. At stake is the cryptographic foundation that secures digital assets like Bitcoin and much of the broader blockchain ecosystem.

Most major blockchains rely on elliptic curve cryptography, a system that ensures private keys cannot feasibly be derived from public addresses using classical computers. However, quantum machines operating with algorithms such as Shor's algorithm could, in theory, break these protections far more efficiently.

"The risk is not immediate, but it is structural," said a digital asset strategist at a large European asset manager. "If quantum computing reaches a certain threshold, it does not just weaken crypto security, it fundamentally challenges it."

Why Quantum Computing Changes The Equation

Unlike incremental technological improvements, quantum computing represents a step change in computational capability.

Traditional computers process information in binary bits, while quantum systems use qubits that can exist in multiple states simultaneously. This allows them to solve certain mathematical problems exponentially faster, particularly those that underpin modern encryption systems.

Companies such as IBM and Google are making steady progress in building more stable and scalable quantum machines. While practical, large-scale systems capable of breaking encryption are not yet available, the trajectory suggests that the risk is no longer purely theoretical.

That shift is beginning to influence how long-term investors think about crypto exposure.

What Happens If Crypto Encryption Breaks

The implications of a quantum breakthrough would be significant, especially for networks that rely on current cryptographic standards.

In Bitcoin's case, a sufficiently advanced quantum computer could theoretically derive private keys from exposed public keys, creating a pathway for unauthorized access to funds. While this scenario remains hypothetical, it represents one of the most widely discussed vulnerabilities.

Importantly, the risk would not be evenly distributed. Wallets that have never revealed their public keys are generally considered more secure, while reused addresses could become more vulnerable.

"There would likely be a transition period rather than an instant collapse," said a blockchain security researcher. "Some funds would be at higher risk than others, and that creates a window where the network would need to respond quickly."

How The Industry Is Preparing

Despite the concerns, the crypto industry is already working on solutions.

Researchers are actively developing quantum-resistant cryptographic methods, often referred to as post-quantum cryptography. These include lattice-based and hash-based systems that are believed to be more resilient against quantum attacks.

Implementing these solutions at scale is complex. Networks like Bitcoin would require broad consensus to adopt new cryptographic standards, a process that can take time. However, experts argue that the incentive to act would be strong if the threat becomes credible.

"There is a perception that Bitcoin cannot adapt, but that is not entirely accurate," said a protocol engineer. "If a real threat emerges, the network has both the technical pathways and economic incentives to upgrade."

In addition to protocol-level changes, transitional strategies are also being explored. These include encouraging users to migrate funds to quantum-resistant addresses and developing hybrid security models that combine classical and post-quantum cryptography.

What It Means For Investors

For investors, quantum computing introduces a new layer of long-term risk analysis.

While the threat is not expected to materialize in the immediate future, its potential impact is significant enough that some institutional players are beginning to factor it into their models. This is particularly relevant for large, long-duration positions in assets like Bitcoin.

"You do not ignore a risk that could theoretically compromise the entire system," said a portfolio manager at a U.S.-based hedge fund. "Even if the timeline is uncertain, the magnitude of the impact demands attention."

At the same time, others argue that the market may be overestimating the immediacy of the threat. Cryptographic systems across the internet, not just in crypto, would need to evolve in response to quantum advancements, suggesting that solutions are likely to develop in parallel.

The growing focus on quantum risk ultimately reflects a broader shift in the crypto market. As the asset class matures, the conversation is expanding beyond price action and adoption metrics to include deeper questions about technological resilience.

For investors, that shift is significant. It signals that crypto is no longer just a speculative asset class, but a system whose long-term viability is being tested against the next generation of technological change.

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: What's Going On With CoreWeave Stock Today?
data.description:
• CoreWeave stock is trading in a tight range. What’s next for CRWV stock?

This news comes as broader markets are experiencing mixed performance, with the Technology sector gaining 0.69%, while major indices, such as the S&P 500 are up 0.21%.

Jane Street has committed approximately $6 billion to use CoreWeave’s AI cloud platform, which underscores the growing demand for high-performance AI capabilities in quantitative trading. Additionally, Jane Street made an equity investment of $1 billion in CoreWeave at a purchase price of $109 per share, reflecting its focus on leveraging machine learning to enhance trading efficiency.

The broader market is seeing gains, with the Technology sector up 0.69% today. CoreWeave’s decline comes as the sector performs well, indicating the stock’s movement may be influenced by specific company factors rather than overall market trends.

Technical Analysis

CoreWeave is currently trading 35.2% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 34.5% above its 50-day SMA, suggesting strong short-term momentum. However, the stock is also trading 13.3% above its 200-day SMA, which indicates a bullish long-term trend, although it has recently shown some bearish signals with the 20-day SMA crossing below the 50-day SMA.

The relative strength index (RSI) is at 74.06, indicating that the stock is overbought, which could suggest a potential pullback in momentum. An RSI above 70 typically signals that a stock may be due for a correction, as buying pressure may be too high.

  • Key Resistance: $120 — This level may act as a barrier for upward movement.
  • Key Support: $114 — A critical level where buying interest may emerge.

CoreWeave is currently underperforming the Technology sector, which is the best-performing sector today, up 0.69%. Over the past 30 days, the Technology sector has gained 7.42%, indicating a strong upward trend, while CoreWeave’s recent performance suggests it is lagging behind its peers.

The stock’s decline today contrasts with the broader sector’s strength, highlighting potential company-specific challenges that may be affecting investor sentiment. As the sector continues to thrive, traders will be watching to see if CoreWeave can regain its footing.

CoreWeave’s agreement with Jane Street not only solidifies CoreWeave’s position in the AI cloud market but also highlights its capability to meet the needs of high-performance computing in quantitative trading. As demand for AI solutions continues to grow, CoreWeave’s innovative offerings place it in a strong position within this rapidly evolving industry.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

CoreWeave is slated to provide its next financial update on May 13 (estimated).

  • EPS Estimate: -123 cents (Down from -60 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $1.96 billion (Up from $981.63 million)
  • Valuation: Forward P/E of N/A

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy rating with a consensus price target of $125.47. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Bernstein: Underperform (Raises target to $67 on April 14)
  • Mizuho: Neutral (Raises target to $105 on April 14)
  • DA Davidson: Buy (Raises target to $175 on April 13)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for CoreWeave, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: Weak (Score: 0.34) — The stock is trading at a steep premium relative to peers.
  • Momentum: Bullish (Score: 93.42) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: CoreWeave’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a momentum-driven story, indicating strong performance in the current market despite its value ranking suggesting a premium valuation.

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because CoreWeave carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows will likely trigger automatic buying or selling of the stock.

Price Action

CRWV Stock Price Activity: CoreWeave shares were down 0.068% at $117.13 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Here's How Much You Would Have Made Owning SPDR Gold MiniShares Trust Stock In The Last 5 Years
data.description:
SPDR Gold MiniShares Trust (NYSE:GLDM) has outperformed the market over the past 5 years by 10.76% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 21.85%. Currently, SPDR Gold MiniShares Trust has a market capitalization of $31.51 billion.

Buying $100 In GLDM: If an investor had bought $100 of GLDM stock 5 years ago, it would be worth $269.85 today based on a price of $95.04 for GLDM at the time of writing.

SPDR Gold MiniShares Trust's Performance Over Last 5 Years

Finally -- what's the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.
